title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connectivity and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide describes Gen Z as Digital Natives who experience life primarily through an online lens. It highlights high daily usage rates of platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and Snapchat, framing constant connectivity as an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens.
70% of students engage with Instagram and Snapchat.
Constant connectivity is a fundamental and indispensable reality in the modern era.

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart comparing digital usage rates with associated mental health outcomes, sourced from the Mental Health Statistics Report 2025. It links high screen time to increased anxiety and depression risk.
key_claims: 87% of individuals use devices more than 2 hours per day.
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor.

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each effect is explained with its impact on mental health and self-perception.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: Fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion.
Comparison: Constant exposure to curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem.
Cyberbullying: Nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions.

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital connectivity: digital detox, user awareness, and platform accountability.
key_claims: Digital Detox: Implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance.
Awareness: Education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant.
Platform Responsibility: Companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative.
